chunks.embed:
## Thesis
Cirrus Logistics is a last-mile freight-brokerage platform mispriced as a cyclical trucker. We believe its asset-light digital brokerage (Cirrus Connect) is structurally taking share, and that gross-margin expansion from automation is underappreciated by the sell side. Base case is a double on a 24-month view; the variant view is that take-rate and Connect attach are durable, not promotional.

chunks.embed:
## Business Overview
Two segments. (1) Brokerage — matching shippers to carriers, ~78% of revenue, historically low-margin. (2) Cirrus Connect — a SaaS + transactional layer (automated load matching, payments, telematics), ~22% of revenue and growing fast. Management guides Connect to 30%+ of revenue by FY28. The variant perception centers on Connect's contribution margin, which the sell-side note models at 12% but which we believe runs closer to 18-20% at scale.
_Financial Summary (USD millions, FY ending Dec)_
| Metric | FY24A | FY25A | FY26E | FY27E |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Revenue | 1,240 | 1,410 | 1,640 | 1,930 |
| Brokerage | 1,010 | 1,120 | 1,250 | 1,400 |
| Cirrus Connect | 230 | 290 | 390 | 530 |
| Gross margin % | 14.1% | 15.0% | 16.4% | 18.1% |
| Adj. EBITDA | 62 | 88 | 131 | 201 |
| Adj. EBITDA margin % | 5.0% | 6.2% | 8.0% | 10.4% |
| Free cash flow | 31 | 47 | 78 | 129 |
| Net debt / EBITDA | 2.1x | 1.6x | 1.0x | 0.4x |

chunks.embed:
## Valuation
CRLG trades at 9.1x FY26E EBITDA vs. asset-light brokerage peers at 13-15x. Our base case applies 13x to FY27E EBITDA of 201, less net debt, for a ~$28 target (vs. $14 today). Bear case (Connect attach stalls, take-rate compresses 150bps) is ~$11; bull case (Connect to 30% of mix by FY27, 20% segment margin) is ~$40.

chunks.embed:
## Key Risks
(1) Freight-cycle downturn compresses brokerage volumes. (2) Connect take-rate is partly promotional and could normalize lower. (3) Customer concentration: top 10 shippers are ~34% of revenue. (4) A large carrier could disintermediate the platform. (5) Capitalized software masks true Connect contribution margin.

chunks.embed:
## Open Diligence Questions (to resolve on the management / sell-side call)
Q1. What is the true contribution margin of Cirrus Connect at current scale, ex-capitalized software? The note models 12%; we think 18-20%.
Q2. Is the Connect take-rate promotional? What is the like-for-like take-rate trend over the last 8 quarters?
- Q3. How concentrated is Connect revenue among the top 10 shippers, and what is net revenue retention?
Q4. What underpins the FY27E gross-margin bridge to 18.1% — mix, automation, or pricing?
Q5. What are the contractual switching costs that prevent a large carrier from disintermediating the platform?
Q6. How sensitive is FY27E EBITDA to a 150bps take-rate compression?
